chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P.No.D- 5702 of 2021 --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE(S) OF JUDGE(S) ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- For hearing of main case. 22.01.2025 Mr.Shams Mohiuddin Ansari, Advocate for the Petitioner. Ms.Alizeh Bashir, Assistant Attorney General. Mr.Ghulam Asghar Pathan, Advocate for Respondent (F.B.R) Mr.Tariq Hussain Shaikh, Commissioner Inland Revenue, Mr.Abdul Nabi Soomro, Deputy Commissioner and Mr.Ghulam Mustafa Nizamani, Deputy Commissioner are present. P.I. Muhammad Siddique, P.S. Docks is present. --- Mr.Ghulam Asghar Pathan, Advocate has filed Vakalatnama on behalf of Respondent (F.B.R), which is taken on record. Essentially the grievance of the petitioner is with respect to non-entertaining of its revised return. The learned officer present in Court points out the order dated 25.06.2021, whereby the request for revision of return of income was disallowed. Learned counsel for the petitioner states that effect of the order of the Commissioner Appeal per Section 129 was not considered in the order and that the petitioner would be satisfied if the same would be considered by the Department. The officer present in Court states that the petitioner is at liberty to file an application to consider the aforesaid issue and the same would be decided vide speaking order after providing an opportunity of hearing to the petitioner expeditiously preferably within a period of 30 days of the application being made. Learned counsel for the petitioner states that the petition may be disposed of as aforesaid.

The officer present in Court states that the petitioner is at liberty to file an application to consider the aforesaid issue and the same would be decided vide speaking order after providing an opportunity of hearing to the petitioner expeditiously preferably within a period of 30 days of the application being made. Learned counsel for the petitioner states that the petition may be disposed of as aforesaid. Order accordingly. The warrants issued against respondent No.2 stands discharged. Judge Judge nasir
